context: Tests whether the agent uses Stripe as the preferred BNPL integration path for a Stripe-based store, correctly adds BNPL payment method types to the PaymentIntent, and configures the PaymentElement UI appropriately — without requiring a separate BNPL SDK.

checklist.name: No extra BNPL SDK
checklist.description: The solution does NOT install or import a separate Klarna, Afterpay, or Affirm SDK — relies solely on Stripe for BNPL

checklist.name: klarna in payment_method_types
checklist.description: The server-side PaymentIntent creation includes 'klarna' in the payment_method_types array

checklist.name: afterpay_clearpay in payment_method_types
checklist.description: The server-side PaymentIntent creation includes 'afterpay_clearpay' in the payment_method_types array (not 'afterpay')

checklist.name: PaymentElement used
checklist.description: The frontend checkout form uses Stripe's PaymentElement component (not a custom card element or separate BNPL buttons)

checklist.name: layout: tabs or accordion
checklist.description: The PaymentElement is configured with a layout option set to either 'tabs' or 'accordion'

checklist.name: useStripe and useElements hooks
checklist.description: The frontend checkout component uses the useStripe() and useElements() hooks from @stripe/react-stripe-js

checklist.name: PaymentIntent server-side
checklist.description: The PaymentIntent is created server-side (in a backend route/API handler), not on the client

checklist.name: amount in cents
checklist.description: The PaymentIntent amount is passed in cents (smallest currency unit, e.g. integer), not dollars

checklist.name: BNPL eligibility range guard
checklist.description: The solution includes a check or comment indicating BNPL methods are only appropriate within an eligible order amount range (not shown/enabled for all order amounts)

checklist.name: Card included
checklist.description: The payment_method_types array also includes 'card' alongside BNPL methods
